name_romaji: Makino Miwatashi Jinja no Kagura Shishimai
name_en: Kagura Lion Dance of Makino Miwatashi Shrine
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a kagura lion dance associated with Makino Miwatashi Shrine. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Okoshi-machi Makino, Horinouchi.
An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a kagura lion dance from Kuride. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Okoshi-machi Kuride, Miyanoshita.
An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a three-lion dance from Kuride. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Okoshi-machi Kuride, Miyanoshita.
An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a kagura lion dance from Tonouchi. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Okoshi-machi Shimo-okoshi, Tonouchi.
An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a three-lion dance from Iwaisawa. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Miyakoji-machi Iwaisawa, Heinaichi.

name_en: Daidai Kagura Sacred Dance of Yamane
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a daidai kagura sacred dance from Yamane. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Tokiwa-machi Tokiwa, Miyanomae.
An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a three-lion dance from Komatsu. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Tokiwa-machi Tokiwa, Nakamachi.

name_en: Married-Couple Lion Dance of Okaburaya Shrine
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a paired (married-couple) lion dance associated with Okaburaya Shrine. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Funehiki-machi Tobudai 6-chome.
An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a three-lion dance from Ishimori. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Funehiki-machi Ishimori, Yato.
An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a three-lion dance from Ishizawa. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Funehiki-machi Ishizawa, Higashi-miyakubo.
An intangible folk cultural property in the form of a three-lion dance from Kodaiji. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Funehiki-machi Ashizawa, Kodaiji.
An intangible folk cultural property in the form of an eight-head lion dance from Ashizawa. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Funehiki-machi Ashizawa, Kamiyakata.
A historic site comprising thirty-three Kannon images at Irimizu. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Takine-machi Sugaya, Dairoku.
A designated historic (archaeological) site at Ishibashi. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Miyakoji-machi Furumichi, Yamaguchi.
A historic site consisting of the ruins of Tokiwa Castle. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Tokiwa-machi Tokiwa, Tate.

name_romaji: Abe Monju Bosatsudō Sandō no Suginamiki
name_en: Cedar Avenue along the Approach to Abe Monju Bosatsu Hall
description_en: A historic site consisting of the cedar tree avenue along the approach to the Abe Monju Bosatsu hall. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Funehiki-machi Monju, Kami.
A historic site comprising thirty-three Kannon images on Mount Katasone. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at the summit of Mount Katasone, Funehiki-machi.

name_en: Sacred Cedar Tree of Usa Shrine
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the sacred cedar (sugi) tree of Usa Shrine. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Takine-machi Hirose, Miyanomae.
A natural monument consisting of the Seikuro mound and a beech (buna) tree. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Takine-machi Kamata, Otakine.
A natural monument consisting of the Abukumado limestone cave. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Takine-machi Sugaya, Higashi-kamayama.

name_romaji: Suwa Jinja no Akagi to Rokugō Shimizu
name_en: Akagi Tree and Rokugo Spring of Suwa Shrine
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the akagi tree and Rokugo spring water at Suwa Shrine. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Takine-machi Hirose, Suwa.

name_en: Weeping Cherry Tree of Gosoji Temple
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the weeping cherry (shidarezakura) tree at Gosoji Temple. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Takine-machi Kamata, Ichinotsubo.
A natural monument consisting of the Benten cherry tree. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Takine-machi Sugaya, Nanamagari.

name_en: Weeping Cherry Tree of Oikazuchi Shrine
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the weeping cherry (shidarezakura) tree at Oikazuchi Shrine. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Takine-machi Kamata, Kinoshita.

name_en: Sacred Grove of Makino Miwatashi Shrine
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the sacred grove of Makino Miwatashi Shrine. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Okoshi-machi Makino, Horinouchi.
A natural monument consisting of the asunaro (hiba cedar) tree at Okame Shrine. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Miyakoji-machi Furumichi, Tatekoshi.

name_en: Cedar Tree of Nishido Kannon Hall
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the cedar (sugi) tree at Nishido Kannon hall. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Miyakoji-machi Iwaisawa, Nishido.
A natural monument consisting of the Nishido grove of hackberry (enoki) and fir (momi) trees. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Miyakoji-machi Iwaisawa, Nishido.

name_en: Japanese White Pine of Amenohiwashi Shrine
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the Japanese white pine (goyomatsu) at Amenohiwashi Shrine. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Miyakoji-machi Iwaisawa, Heinaichi.

name_en: Umbrella Pine of the Former Furumichi Elementary School
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the umbrella-shaped pine (kasamatsu) at the former Furumichi Elementary School. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Miyakoji-machi Furumichi, Kitamachi.
A natural monument consisting of the cherry tree at Ashibisawa. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Miyakoji-machi Iwaisawa, Ashibisawa.
A natural monument consisting of the sowing-season (tanemaki) cherry tree of Nakasaku. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Miyakoji-machi Iwaisawa, Nakasaku.
A natural monument consisting of the Abumizuri-ishi cherry tree at Oise-sama. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Tokiwa-machi Wasegawa, Ipponmatsu.
A natural monument consisting of the great ginkgo tree at Kayama Shrine. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Tokiwa-machi Kayama, Waseda.
A natural monument consisting of the great cherry tree at Daikokuten. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Tokiwa-machi Kubo, Tochikubo.
A natural monument consisting of the male cedar (osugi) tree at Hiwashi Shrine. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Tokiwa-machi Hotta, Shin'yashiki.
A natural monument consisting of the Wasegawa-date cherry tree. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Tokiwa-machi Tokiwa, Wasegawa-date.
A natural monument consisting of the kaya (Japanese nutmeg-yew) tree at Chohoji Temple. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Funehiki-machi Kamiutsushi, Machi.

name_romaji: Kumano Daigongendō no Sugi to Fuji
name_en: Cedar and Wisteria of Kumano Daigongen Hall
description_en: A natural monument consisting of the cedar (sugi) tree and wisteria (fuji) at Kumano Daigongen hall. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2005-04-18. Located at Funehiki-machi Ashizawa, Tate.

name_romaji: Seisatsu oyobi Ashizawa Hanatate Seisatsuba Ato
name_en: Public Notice Board and Site of the Ashizawa Hanatate Notice Board Station
description_en: A tangible cultural property and historic site comprising a public notice board (seisatsu) and the site of the Ashizawa Hanatate notice-board station. Municipally designated by Tamura City on 2006-07-10. Located at Funehiki-machi Ashizawa, Hanatate-mae and vicinity.
